evals.prompt: I want to add authentication to my app. Can you help me write a spec and a plan?
evals.expected_output: Skill runs the spec interview (asks about goal, scope, constraints, interface, success criteria), scaffolds both plan/<name>.specs.md and plan/<name>.plan.md, authors a Contract-level spec with at minimum Goal/Requirements/Interfaces sections, runs validate.py --spec, runs sync.py to populate plan stubs with Satisfies: fields, then runs validate.py --cross to confirm coverage.
expectations: Skill asks at least one clarifying question before scaffolding (spec interview step)
scaffold.py is run to produce both paired files
Produced spec includes a Goal section with one sentence and a measurable completion signal
Produced spec includes at least one SEC-### requirement
sync.py is run to populate plan stubs — plan contains Satisfies: fields
validate.py --cross is run and coverage matrix is reported
Skill does not jump straight to implementation or coder

evals.prompt: Write a contract-level spec and plan for a REST API endpoint: POST /api/v1/users that creates a new user account. Request body: email (string, required) and password (string, required, min 8 chars). Returns the created user object (id, email, createdAt). Requires Bearer token auth. Must respond within 300ms. Cannot use the existing users_legacy table.
evals.expected_output: A complete Contract-level spec (all 7 mandatory sections, validate.py --spec returns 0 errors) paired with a plan (sync.py populated Satisfies: fields, validate.py --cross returns 0 errors, coverage matrix shows all REQs covered).
expectations: Spec includes all 7 mandatory Contract sections: Goal, Requirements, Constraints, Interfaces, Context, Acceptance Criteria & Validation, Examples & Edge Cases
PERF-### requirement captures the 300ms threshold
SEC-### requirement captures Bearer token auth
CON-### constraint captures the users_legacy table exclusion
Interfaces section includes 400, 401, and at least one 5xx error case
validate.py --spec is run with --level contract and returns 0 errors
sync.py is run and plan contains Satisfies: fields mapping tasks to REQ/SEC/PERF IDs
validate.py --cross returns 0 errors with full coverage matrix
Reviewer subagent is spawned after cross-validation passes

evals.prompt: /planning --depth blueprint "High-throughput event ingestion pipeline. Events via HTTP POST, queued in Redis, processed into PostgreSQL. Must handle 10,000 events/second, survive Redis restarts without data loss, and roll back cleanly if the PostgreSQL schema migration fails."
evals.expected_output: A complete Blueprint-level spec (all 8 sections, Notes & Risks with Mermaid diagram, rollback strategy) paired with a blueprint plan including a PHASE-ROLLBACK section. Both files validated; validate.py --cross reports 0 errors.
expectations: Spec includes all 8 mandatory Blueprint sections including Notes & Risks
Notes & Risks contains a Mermaid diagram (graph TD or sequenceDiagram)
Notes & Risks contains at least one rollback strategy entry
PERF-### requirement captures 10,000 events/second threshold
At least one RISK-### addresses Redis restart / data loss
validate.py --spec is run with --level blueprint and returns 0 errors
Plan includes a rollback phase
validate.py --cross returns 0 errors

evals.expected_output: Skill skips spec authoring, runs sync.py on the existing spec to generate plan stubs with Satisfies: fields, then validates with validate.py --plan and validate.py --cross.
expectations: Skill does not re-author or overwrite the existing spec file
sync.py is run on the existing spec to populate plan/<name>.plan.md
Plan contains Satisfies: fields
validate.py --plan and validate.py --cross are run
Skill reports coverage matrix before handoff

evals.prompt: Create a plan for adding JWT authentication to an Express API
evals.expected_output: Skill recognizes this as a planning request and runs the full pipeline producing both plan/<name>.specs.md and plan/<name>.plan.md (not just a plan file). Plan tasks have Satisfies: fields and validate.py --cross returns a clean coverage matrix.
expectations: Both spec and plan artifacts are produced
Plan tasks include Satisfies: fields
validate.py is run in --cross mode
Coverage matrix shows all requirements covered

evals.prompt: /planning --spec-only "Define the API contract for a password reset flow: user submits email, receives a time-limited reset link, clicks link to set a new password."
evals.expected_output: Skill produces only plan/<name>.specs.md (Contract depth by default). No plan file is created. validate.py --spec is run and returns 0 errors. Skill explicitly stops at Step 3 and does not proceed to sync.py or plan authoring.
expectations: Only the spec file (plan/<name>.specs.md) is written — no plan file
scaffold.py is run to create the spec file
validate.py --spec is run and returns 0 errors
Skill stops after spec authoring and does not invoke sync.py or validate.py --plan
Skill communicates clearly that --spec-only mode stopped before planning

evals.expected_output: Skill outputs the exact error message: 'Spec file not found: plan/nonexistent.specs.md. Create it first or omit --from-spec.' and stops without creating any files.
expectations: Skill does not create any new files
Skill outputs the specified error message
Skill does not proceed to scaffold.py or sync.py

evals.prompt: Create a spec and plan for a REST API endpoint. Use the goal 'Make a simple, robust user registration API'. If validate.py reports any warnings or errors, modify the spec to resolve them and re-validate.
evals.expected_output: Skill detects vague adjectives like 'simple' and 'robust', replaces them with precise, measurable requirements (e.g. numeric latency limits), runs validate.py --spec, and returns 0 warnings.
expectations: Skill runs validate.py --spec to validate the initial draft
Skill detects/warns about vague adjectives like 'simple' and 'robust' in the goal
Skill corrects the goal/requirements to use specific, measurable thresholds
Skill runs validate.py --spec successfully on the revised spec with 0 warnings

evals.prompt: We have an existing spec plan/auth.specs.md with two requirements: REQ-001 (JWT issuance) and REQ-002 (rejecting invalid token). Now add a new requirement: SEC-001 (tokens must expire in 3600s). Re-sync the plan and show the updated cross-validation matrix.
evals.expected_output: Skill runs sync.py to add the new stub for SEC-001 without manual modifications to Satisfies fields, and verifies with validate.py --cross.
expectations: Skill does not manually edit the Satisfies: field of the tasks
Skill runs sync.py to add the new stub for SEC-001
Skill runs validate.py --cross to check the updated coverage

evals.prompt: Upgrade the exploratory sketch spec/plan plan/payment-sketch (contains REQ-001, REQ-002) to blueprint level. Add Notes & Risks, a Mermaid diagram, and a database schema interface definition, and add a rollback phase to the plan.
evals.expected_output: Skill adds Notes & Risks section with Mermaid diagram, defines database schema in Interfaces, validates spec at blueprint depth, and appends PHASE-ROLLBACK to the plan.
expectations: Skill adds a Notes & Risks section with a valid Mermaid diagram
Skill runs validate.py --spec --level blueprint and returns 0 errors
Skill appends a rollback phase (PHASE-ROLLBACK) with appropriate tasks to the plan
